---
title: "Project Name"
author: 
  - name: First Last # replace with your name
    email: myemail@domain.com # Optional: Delete this line if you prefer not to share your email.
    
date: YYYY-MM-DD # adjust to today’s date
date-format: long
image: "/images/vincent-van-zalinge-mDohQISBnCk-unsplash.jpg" # In your local copy of the Nexus repository, add a representative image for this resource to the images folder. Both PNGs and JPGs are acceptable formats. Adjust the filename here from "vincent-van-zalinge-mDohQISBnCk-unsplash.jpg" to the name of your image.
categories: 
  - Projects # always include
  - Domain # E.g., Computer vision, NLP, Geospatial, Data augmentation, Preprocessing
  - Language # E.g., Python, R, SAS, Ruby
  - Related frameworks used by library # E.g., PyTorch, Tensorflow
  - Task # E.g., Wrangling, Text analysis, Data visualization,
  - Other useful tags # optional E.g., Open-Source, Contributors Wanted

---
## About {{< meta title >}}

{{< meta title >}} is a [brief description of the library: its purpose and key features]. Developed by [Author(s)/Organization] 
and introduced in [Year], {{< meta title >}} offers tools for [specific task(s), e.g., image transformations, data augmentation, visualization]. 
Its design allows users to [mention ease of use, efficiency, flexibility], making it ideal for [target audience or task, e.g., deep learning practitioners, data engineers, etc.].

#### Key features
<!-- MARKDOWN COMMENT: List out features of the project. Even if they haven't been developed yet, it is nice to give 
potential users and contributors what is expected of the project. Try and stay away from stating that something is or 
is not implemented yet. This means you will need to come back and update your project on the UW RSE Nexus or it will 
become out of date.
-->

- **Feature 1**: Describe a core feature, 
- **Feature 2**: Another key feature of the project

## Integration and compatibility
{{< meta title >}} integrates with various machine learning frameworks and libraries, making it versatile for a range of tasks.

- **Frameworks Supported**: List supported frameworks, e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn
- **Compatible Libraries**: Mention compatible libraries, e.g., OpenCV, Hugging Face Transformers
- **Installation Instructions**: Provide installation commands, e.g., `pip install library-name`, `conda install library-name`

## Use cases
Here are some examples of how {{< meta title >}} can be applied.

- **Use Case 1**: Description of a specific use case, 
- **Use Case 2**: Another relevant application, 
- **Use Case 3**: Optional: Add more use cases if applicable

## Tutorials and resources
#### Getting started

- **[Official Tutorial](URL)**: Link to a beginner-friendly tutorial or notebook
- **[Example Colab/Notebook](URL)**: Link to an example Colab notebook or GitHub repository showcasing the library in action

#### High-level tips for effective use
- **Optimization**: Describe ways to optimize performance, 
- **Memory Management**: Tips on handling memory usage, especially for large datasets
- **Common Pitfalls**: Mention common mistakes and how to avoid them

### Related libraries & tools
- **Related Library 1**: Mention a related library and what it adds/compares to the current library
- **Tool/Plugin 1**: Mention tools/plugins that complement this library

## Contribute
### Repository

- **[Contribute to the source code](URL)**: Link to [Github/GitLab/etc.] repository.

<!-- MARKDOWN COMMENT: Some other contribution headings and shoutouts might be desired for some projects, especially 
in cases where contributions may not be possible directly to source code. -->
### Resources
Please feel free to write a guide that would be helpful for new or advanced users of {{< meta title >}}.

- **[Create a Guide](/KB/Guides/How-to-contribute.qmd)**: Create a guide for {{< meta title >}} on the UW RSE Nexus.

## Questions?
If you have any lingering questions about this resource, feel free to post them on the [UW RSE Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/UW-RSE-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub.

## See also
<!-- MARKDOWN COMMENT: Please Check the existing resources on Nexus to see if any other related resources 
(e.g., related books/videos, blog posts commenting on the resource, alternative approaches/frameworks, etc.) 
should be linked below. You may also link to resources which aren't currently on the Nexus platform, if applicable. 
However, if you're feeling ambitious, you may wish to post those to Nexus as well!  
NOTE: When doing related resources links to UW RSE Nexus pages, please use local pathing to the .qmd file instead
of the web url. So, use "/KB/Guides/some_guide.qmd" instead of the "https://.../some_guide.html" URL.
-->
- [Related Resource 1](Link to related resource 1): Brief description of related resource 1.
- [Related Resource 2](Link to related resource 2): Brief description of related resource 2.
- [Related Resource 3](Link to related resource 3): Brief description of related resource 3
